steps.title: Check the sentence boundary
steps.content: The first clause says the djeser and heqat were ancient Egyptian units of measurement. The next idea begins with a modifier, Used to record length and volume, respectively, and then continues with a complete clause about the units falling out of use.

steps.title: Avoid comma splices
steps.content: A and B incorrectly use only a comma to connect complete clauses. D creates an awkward and unparallel structure. A period after measurement cleanly separates the sentence before the modifying phrase. The answer is C.
